executive_summary: The teacher demonstrates strong presence and alertness. Key areas for growth include refining gesture size to match emphasis and ensuring a more deliberate, even room scan. Additionally, reducing extended note-reading and softening facial tone could enhance connection.

top_strengths.title: Alert room presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room. 00:15-00:30 kept the head and eyes visibly engaged with the room.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.

top_strengths.title: Upright confident presence
top_strengths.evidence: 00:00-00:15 held an upright, settled stance rather than a collapsed or guarded one. 00:15-00:30 held an upright, settled stance rather than a collapsed or guarded one.
top_strengths.what_to_repeat: Keep the same upright, settled stance between points and transitions.

priority_actions.title: Tighten the peak size of gestures
priority_actions.why_it_matters: Large bursts of motion can distract from the teaching point when they are not tightly timed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required. 00:15-00:30 had gesture peaks that looked larger than the teaching point required.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: Keep big gestures for true emphasis and use smaller controlled hand movements elsewhere.

priority_actions.title: Deliberately sweep the room
priority_actions.why_it_matters: More even attention across the room helps students feel included and keeps engagement distributed.
priority_actions.what_we_saw: 00:00-00:15 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep. 00:15-00:30 stayed more fixed on one part of the room than on a left-center-right sweep.
priority_actions.what_to_try_next: At each major point, pause and sweep left-center-right before moving on.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Soften the visible facial tone
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:30-00:45 showed a flatter or tighter visible facial tone than the rest of the clip.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.

additional_observation_inventory.title: Reduce extended note-reading
additional_observation_inventory.evidence: 00:30-00:45 repeatedly pulled attention down to notes instead of back to the room.
additional_observation_inventory.suggested_response: Check whether note glances stay brief and return to the room within the same teaching beat.

keep_doing: Keep the same quick room checks that make the lecture feel attentive.
Keep the same upright, settled stance between points and transitions.
watch_for: Check whether gesture size matches the importance of the point instead of peaking on routine lines.
Check whether the next explanation visibly reaches more than one side of the room.
Check whether the face resets between points instead of staying tight through the whole sentence.
Check whether note glances stay brief and return to the room within the same teaching beat.

evidence_moments.headline: Uneven Room Scan
evidence_moments.observed_behavior: uneven room scan / over animated delivery / open palm explaining
evidence_moments.metric_evidence: Overall 50.6; room scan 50.1; presence 80.5; natural movement 24.9.
evidence_moments.qwen_interpretation: Qwen sees the teacher mainly addressing the audience; open-palm explanatory gestures recur; stance is often static.
evidence_moments.coaching_implication: At each major point, pause and sweep left-center-right before moving on.

evidence_moments.headline: Distributed Room Engagement

evidence_moments.metric_evidence: Overall 64.9; room scan 60.3; presence 90.5; natural movement 61.9.
evidence_moments.qwen_interpretation: Qwen sees the teacher mainly addressing the audience; stance is often static.
evidence_moments.coaching_implication: Reuse the left-center-right room sweep that already looks natural.

evidence_moments.headline: Note Reading
evidence_moments.observed_behavior: over animated delivery / tense or neutral affect / note reading
evidence_moments.metric_evidence: Overall 54.3; room scan 66.3; presence 88.1; natural movement 18.4.
evidence_moments.qwen_interpretation: Qwen sees repeated note-reading or notes-focused attention; stance is often static; posture looks closed or slouched in several samples.
evidence_moments.coaching_implication: Raise notes closer to eye level and rehearse short glance-return cycles during key explanations.
